mission.title: kube-ovn: [Feature Request] Announce NAT-GW EIP over BGP
mission.description: [Feature Request] Announce NAT-GW EIP over BGP. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kube-ovn deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kube-ovn version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-ovn
helm list -n kube-system 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kube-ovn installation.

steps.title: Review kube-ovn configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kube-ovn configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-ovn
kubectl get configmap -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kube-ovn
```
When adding EIPs from an "external subnet", the act of receiving the traffic for our EIPs is ensured by ARP on the L2 segment between the "external subnet" GW and the NAT-GW from Kube-OVN.
A more suited way of announcing those IPs would be BGP.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature Request] Announce NAT-GW EIP over BGP
steps.description:
# Pull Request

## What type of this PR

Examples of user facing changes:
- Introduces a flag to the BGP speaker to turn it into a "NAT GW mode", this makes the speaker announce EIPs linked to the GW the speaker is currently in. This disables announcement of Pod IPs/Services/Subnets by the speaker.
```yaml
apiVersion: "k8s.cni.cncf.io/v1"
kind: NetworkAttachmentDefinition
metadata:
  name: ovn-nad
  namespace: default
spec:
  config: '{
      "cniVersion": "0.3.0",
      "type": "kube-ovn",
      "server_socket": "/run/openvswitch/kube-ovn-daemon.sock",
      "provider": "ovn-nad.default.ovn"
    }'
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-ovn
kubectl get events -n kube-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature Request] Announce NAT-GW EIP over BGP" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
# Pull Request

## What type of this PR

Examples of user facing changes:
- Introduces a flag to the BGP speaker to turn it into a "NAT GW mode", this makes the speaker announce EIPs linked to the GW the speaker is currently in. This disables announcement of Pod IPs/Services/Subnets by the speaker.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: "k8s.cni.cncf.io/v1"
kind: NetworkAttachmentDefinition
metadata:
  name: ovn-nad
  namespace: default
spec:
  config: '{
      "cniVersion": "0.3.0",
      "type": "kube-ovn",
      "server_socket": "/run/openvswitch/kube-ovn-daemon.sock",
      "provider": "ovn-nad.default.ovn"
    }'

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  labels:
    kubernetes.io/bootstrapping: rbac-defaults
  name: system:vpc-nat-gw
rules:
  - apiGroups:
    - ""
    resources:
    - services
    - pods
    verbs:
    - list
    - watch
  - apiGroups:
    - kubeovn.io
    resources:
    - iptables-eips
    - subnets
    - vpc-nat-gateways
    verbs:
    - list
    - watch
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  annotations:
    rbac.authorization.kubernetes.io/autoupdate: "true"
  labels:
    kubernetes.io/bootstrapping: rbac-defaults
  name: vpc-nat-gw
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: system:vpc-nat-gw
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: vpc-nat-gw
  namespace: kube-system
---
api

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with Kube-OVN installed as the CNI plugin.
